mini-project.label: Mini-Project: End-to-End Query
mini-project.summary: Combine all skills in a small project to answer business questions.

step1-select-products.title: Step 1: Select Products Over a Price Threshold

step2-sort-products.title: Step 2: Sort Products by Price Descending

intro-mini-project.title: Mini-Project Overview
intro-mini-project.bodyMarkdown: In this mini-project, you'll use your SQL skills to answer a real-world business question. You'll work step-by-step, integrating concepts like filtering, sorting, aggregation, and joining tables. This hands-on challenge will help you see how SQL is used to solve practical problems.

step1-select-products.title: Step 1: Select Products Over a Price Threshold
step1-select-products.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the `products` table in the `products_catalog` dataset where the price is greater than 50.
step1-select-products.hint: Filter products by price using WHERE.

help.concept: The WHERE clause is used to filter rows based on a condition. Use it to select only products with a price greater than 50.
help.hint_1: Remember to use WHERE with the price column and the greater than operator.
help.hint_2: Your query should select all columns from products where price is above 50.

step2-sort-products.title: Step 2: Sort Products by Price Descending
step2-sort-products.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the `products` table and sort the results by price in descending order.
step2-sort-products.hint: Use ORDER BY with DESC to sort.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts the result set. DESC sorts in descending order.
help.hint_1: Add ORDER BY price DESC to your query.
help.hint_2: Make sure you are selecting all columns and sorting by price from highest to lowest.

step2-sort-products.starterCode:
-- Sort products by price descending
SELECT

step3-count-products-category.title: Step 3: Count Products by Category
step3-count-products-category.prompt: Write a SQL query to count the number of products in each category from the `products` table.

help.concept: GROUP BY groups rows by a column, and COUNT counts the number of rows in each group.
help.hint_1: Select the category and use COUNT(*), then group by category.
help.hint_2: Your query should show each category with the number of products in that category.

step4-join-products-inventory.title: Step 4: Join Products with Inventory
step4-join-products-inventory.prompt: Write a SQL query to join the `products` table with the `inventory` table (both in the `products_catalog` dataset) on the product id, and select the product name and inventory quantity.
step4-join-products-inventory.hint: Use JOIN on product id columns.

help.concept: A JOIN combines rows from two tables based on a related column. Use ON to specify the join condition.
help.hint_1: Join products and inventory using the product id columns.
help.hint_2: Select the product name from products and quantity from inventory after joining.
